hook: URA's latest moves are quietly reshaping where Singapore's value flows next

slides.sub: The zoning changes and policy signals most buyers miss — and what they actually mean for your next move.

slides.lead: URA's masterplan isn't a map — it's a price signal hiding in plain sight.

slides.pre: Historical uplift on rezoned residential sites
slides.post: Median land value premium observed within 3 years of URA use-type upgrade, based on GLS and en bloc precedents.
slides.source: URA GLS data · POV Guy analysis

items.body: Higher-density overlays in selected Outside Central Region nodes compress suburban price gaps with CCR.
Long-term Rail Corridor and green buffer designations lock out development — protecting adjacent landed enclaves.
White site releases near MRT hubs signal where the next wave of mixed-use supply — and rental demand — lands.

items.label: District 22 (Jurong)
items.sub: Jurong Lake District — long-term CBD2 play

slides.label: The angle most miss
slides.punch: Most buyers look at today's zoning. Sophisticated buyers look at what URA signals through the white sites, infrastructure spend, and density allowances — 5 to 10 years out.
slides.punch_bold: Buy the trajectory, not the postcode.

items.body: MAS held TDSR at 55% — no cooling-measure relief yet, sustaining a cautious buyer posture across all segments.
HDB BTO supply ramped up in non-mature estates; could moderate suburban condo demand for 24–36 months.
ABSD remains at 60% for foreigners — Sentosa and CCR investment demand stays structurally suppressed near-term.

slides.pre: Eyeing a district on the masterplan?
slides.title: Let's map your
slides.sub: 30-min strategy call. We'll run the zoning overlay, the supply pipeline, and the price trajectory — before you commit.

slides.title: Forward this to
slides.sub: If you know anyone shortlisting a district this quarter, send them this before they sign anything.

caption:
URA's masterplan is public — but most buyers read it wrong. 📈

• Rezoned residential sites have historically printed ~30% land value uplift within 3 years
• Jurong Lake District, Punggol Digital District, and Geylang are the districts most exposed to long-term density uplift
• ABSD at 60% and TDSR at 55% mean the policy floor isn't moving — position around infrastructure, not sentiment

The map is free. The read is where the edge is. If you're shortlisting any district in the next 90 days, the zoning picture matters more than the showflat brochure.
